issue_title: Support Vector Machine with Pulp
issue_body:
Hi,
I'm trying to compare SVM solve with Pulp vs Scikit-learn using the following LP problem:
![equation](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30179790/28216933-49697dc8-68ab-11e7-8365-ae55e1118a51.jpg)
Unfortunately, Pulp return some value for **b** and **Eta**, but **Alpha** and **a** are stuck to zero.
I'm question the way I implemented it as I can't find the constrains with the Kernel matrix in the LP file...  

# SVM implementation in Pulp vs Scikit-learn

    # define the hyper parameters
    C = 10
    eps = 0.1

    # define the variables
    RANGE = list(range(N))
    alpha = pulp.LpVariable.dicts("alpha", (RANGE,), cat='Continuous')
    b = pulp.LpVariable("b", cat='Continuous')
    a = pulp.LpVariable.dicts("a", (RANGE,), lowBound=0, cat='Continuous')
    Eta = pulp.LpVariable.dicts("Eta", (RANGE,), lowBound=0, cat='Continuous')

    prob = LpProblem("Constrained SVM", LpMinimize)

    # implementing the constrains to Pulp
    for i in range(N):
        prob += lpDot(alpha, K[i]) + b - y[i] >= -Eta[i]
        prob += lpDot(alpha, K[i]) + b - y[i] <= Eta[i]
        prob += eps >= 0
        prob += eps <= Eta[i]
        prob += alpha[i] <= a[i]
        prob += alpha[i] >= -a[i]

    # call the objective function
    prob += lpSum(a) + C * lpSum(Eta)

    prob.writeLP("MySVM.lp")
    prob.solve()

    print("Status:", LpStatus[prob.status])

    #storing Optimisation result
    varDic = {}
    for v in prob.variables():
        if v.name[:5] == 'alpha' or v.name == 'b':
            varDic[v.name] = v.varValue

    #passing solution (alpha, beta) to proper ndarray
    b = varDic['b']
    del varDic['b']

    alp = np.zeros(N)
    for key in varDic:
        alp[int(key[6:])] = varDic[key]


    #clean up PULP variables
    alpha.clear()
    Eta.clear()
    a.clear()
    varDic.clear()

    #Projection of the solution from Pulp and Sci-learn
    y_pulp = np.zeros(N)
    for i in range(N):
        y_pulp[i] = np.dot(alp, K[i, :]) + b

    svr_rbf = SVR(kernel='rbf', C=1e3, gamma=0.1)
    y_rbf = svr_rbf.fit(x, y).predict(x)

    #display result
    lw = 2
    plt.scatter(x, y, color='darkorange', label='data')
    plt.hold('on')
    plt.plot(x, y_rbf, color='navy', lw=lw, label='SciLearn')
    plt.plot(x, y_pulp, color='cornflowerblue', lw=lw, label='Pulp')
    plt.xlabel('data')
    plt.ylabel('target')
    plt.title('Support Vector Regression')
    plt.legend()
    plt.show()

issue_title: How to use external data in the objective function?
issue_body:
I want to select a square subset of a square matrix, such that the sum of the subset matrix is minimised. Some code:

```{python}
import nump as np
import pulp

def subset_matrix(data, inds):
    return data[np.ix_(inds, inds)]

A = np.random.random((10, 10))  # replace with covariance matrix

inds = list(range(len(A)))

prob = pulp.LpProblem("Minimum subset", pulp.LpMaximize)

x = pulp.LpVariable.dicts('elem', inds, lowBound=0, upBound=1, cat=pulp.LpInteger)

prob += pulp.lpSum(subset_cov(A, [x[i] for i in inds]))

prob.solve()
```

This fails because numpy indexing doesn't like the `inds` being a list of `LpVariables`. Is there a way around this?

issue_title: Split user-data into separate files for each service
issue_body:
See https://github.com/deis/deis/pull/3716#issuecomment-106017374

Basically, the user-data file is unruly. Adding new units or files to this is difficult to manage.

I'd like to explore creating a new Makefile target like `combine-user-data` which would simply combine all of these files together. `make discovery-url` could depend on this target before it replaces the discovery URL. 

Note that we need to be careful that we don't break the provision scripts in this. I think we should be fine as long as we still ensure users are running `make discovery-url` before running the provision scripts, since this target would also combine the user data pieces.

issue_title: app routes return 404 after all instances are down
issue_body:
Instead of a 503, `deis open` is returning a 404 for an existing app with all web apps scaled down to zero. ``` ><> cd example-ruby-sinatra/ ><> deis ps === ruby Processes ><> curl http://ruby.local3.deisapp.com 404 Not Found

404 Not Found

nginx/1.9.0
``` I think this occurs because we're using `lsdir` instead of `ls` in [the router template](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/router/image/templates/nginx.conf#L162), which only iterates through all subkeys which also have subkeys. When all processes are scaled down, all subkeys are expired, therefore the router template removes the entry.

issue_title: Escape invalid json characters before returning over json-rpc
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Some valid string characters, like newline, are invalid in json encoding. This is doesn't seem to be handled by `json.dumps()` with the default encoder. If an exception is triggered in RPC, and that exception message has a newline, then clients who try to naively decode with `json.loads()` will crash.


### How can it be fixed

Handle all of the invalid characters from ECMA-404 as mentioned in [this post](https://stackoverflow.com/a/42899451/8412986):

> According to spec: http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf
>
> >A string is a sequence of Unicode code points wrapped with quotation marks
(U+0022). All characters may be placed within the quotation marks except for the
characters that must be escaped: quotation mark (U+0022), reverse solidus
(U+005C), and the control characters U+0000 to U+001F. There are two-character
escape sequence representations of some characters.
>
>So you can't pass 0x0A or 0x0C codes directly. It is forbidden! Spec suggests to use escape sequences for some well defined codes from U+0000 to U+001F:
>
> > \f  represents the form feed character (U+000C). 
> > \n  represents the line feed character (U+000A).

issue_title: Implement p2p discovery v5
issue_body: The discovery v4 protocol is really bad at finding LES peers, so we had to resort to hard-coding known LES peers in the `PeerPool` we use with our `LightChain`. The latest release of geth (1.8.x) implements the [discovery v5 protocol](https://github.com/fjl/p2p-drafts), though, which is much better at finding peers that support a specific sub-protocol (e.g. LES), so we should implement that (probably even dropping v4) and change our `PeerPool` to use `p2p.DiscoveryProtocol` instead of hard-coded nodes.

issue_title: Feature request: packages list configuration export for sharing and backup.
issue_body:
Hi there,
I haven't touched Anaconda for a few months due to other works and commitments. 

Is there any way to export a list of all install package and configuration for importation at another machine?

This is useful when working in a classroom where people use different versions of python, provided that we can use the older packages, it should be available for education purpose. 

Regards

issue_title: `conda install /path/to/some_pkg-1.0.0-0.tar.bz` doesn't upgrade package
issue_body:
If a package is installed and you re-install again with a tarball a new `conda-meta/` file is created which makes your `conda list` look odd: the same package will be installed N times.

This would be particularly harmful for changes where a file was removed.

issue_title: scipy 0.16.1-np110py27_p0 [mkl] wants to update the wrong direction
issue_body:
Under python 2.7.11 with mkl, I ran `conda search --outdated`, and noticed that many packages were out of date. I manually updated several which at one point caused a downgrade to 2.7.10, and then upgrade back to 2.7.11 (once this happened once, it didn't do it again).

issue_title: activate/deactivate as conda commands
issue_body:
Hi,

I use conda environment a lot, and I wish `activate`/`deactivate` are actual conda subcommands, not separate scripts. At least to me, after creating an environment with `conda create`, switching to another environment with `conda activate` and `conda deactivate` would be very intuitive.

Thanks a lot for the great utility!

issue_title: wrong install directory in boost 1.57.0 for Python 3.5 on Windows
issue_body:
Executing the following on Windows:
```
conda create -n python3 python=3.5
activate python3
conda install boost
```
installs the boost DLLs in `$CONDA_ACTIVE_ENV/Library/lib` instead of `$CONDA_ACTIVE_ENV/Library/bin` where they belong.

issue_title: Packages from repo.continuum.io have the same name and version, but different MD5 checksums
issue_body:
Occasionally, I have packages--originally from repo.continuum.io--whose MD5 sum does *not* match the current package of the same name and version.

Can the build number be bumped whenever packages undergo whatever transformation is making them yield a different MD5 sum (but doesn't require a version bump)?

This breaks many things that cache packages.

issue_title: --force does NOT imply --no-deps (anymore?)
issue_body:
Using conda 4.4.7, `conda install --force` does not seem to imply `--no-deps`, even so it says in the docstring:

```bash
-f, --force           Force install (even when package already installed),
                        implies --no-deps.
```

Here I try only `--force`:

```bash
(stable) └─❱❱❱ conda install -f hdf5=1.8                                                                                              +8947 16:46 ❰─┘
Solving environment: done

## Package Plan ##

  environment location: /Users/klay6683/miniconda3/envs/stable

  added / updated specs:
    - hdf5=1.8


The following packages will be UPDATED:

    libdap4:   3.18.3-2     conda-forge --> 3.19.2-1             conda-forge
    libpq:     9.6.3-0      conda-forge --> 9.6.6-h77f6c7a_0

The following packages will be DOWNGRADED:

    gdal:      2.2.3-py36_0 conda-forge --> 2.2.2-py36hd505dc6_1
    h5py:      2.7.1-py36_2 conda-forge --> 2.7.1-py36_1         conda-forge
    hdf5:      1.10.1-1     conda-forge --> 1.8.18-2             conda-forge
    kealib:    1.4.7-4      conda-forge --> 1.4.7-3              conda-forge

    netcdf4:   1.3.1-py36_1 conda-forge --> 1.3.1-py36_0         conda-forge

As you can see, it tries to downgrade the dependencies.

Using `--no-deps`, things work as expected:

Running on macOS 10.12.6.

issue_title: JSON output for errors should be properly formatted instead of text
issue_body:
Here's example output:

``` json
{
  "error": "No packages found in current osx-64 channels matching: ipython-notebookzzz\n\nDid you mean one of these?\n\n    ipython-notebook\n\nYou can search for this package on Binstar with\n\n    binstar search -t conda ipython-notebookzzz\n\nYou may need to install the Binstar command line client with\n\n    conda install binstar",
  "error_type": null
}
```

Note all of the new lines.  There's valid structured data hidden in there, but it requires that I write a text processor in each environment I want to use it in.  Here's an alternative:

``` json
{
  "error": "No packages found in current osx-64 channels matching: ipython-notebookzzz",
  "packageName": "ipython-notebookzzz",
  "channels": [
    "http://example.com/foo/",
    "http://example.com/bar/"
  ],
  "platform": "osx-64",
  "alternatives": [
    "ipython-notebook"
  ]
}
```

Note, this problem is created by the `conda.cli` modules providing formatted text data rather than raw python data structures to an output/render function that then handles formatting the display depending on the context (see [these lines](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/41607615d422a3b20c9a485bf070c3cdd7c29de2/conda/cli/install.py#L355-L371) for an example).  To make these changes we'll either need to refactor `common.error_and_exit` or write a text parser inside it.

issue_title: conda skeleton picked oldest version instead of newest
cannot set unlisted parameters
issue_body:
conda config --set update_dependencies false

fails with:

    CondaValueError: Key 'update_dependencies' is not a known primitive parameter.

Appears to be a regression in 4.3.16 caused by https://github.com/conda/conda/pull/4913 , which restricts `--set` to only those parameters not in the UNLISTED_PARAMETERS set.


The parameter exists and appears to be respected, but now must be manually set via editing config files. While attempting to look into this, I created a crash in conda prompting the following message:

An unexpected error has occurred. Conda has prepared the above report.
Would you like conda to send this report to the core maintainers?
[y/N]:
No report sent. To permanently opt-out, use

    $ conda config --set report_errors false
```

But the given command fails with the same error.

issue_title: Further refine conda channels specification.
issue_body:
A valid repo must minimally contain a file (even if blank) `noarch/repodata.json` and `noarch/repodata.json.bz2`.  Right now, conda assumes that the platform directory for a channel always exists, and it causes errors when it doesn't.  We'll likely be adding new platforms soon, and noarch is/should be in all cases the common `subdir`.  anaconda.org is already compatible:


* https://conda.anaconda.org/kalefranz/noarch/repodata.json
* https://conda.anaconda.org/kalefranz/label/main/noarch/repodata.json
* https://conda.anaconda.org/kalefranz/label/NOPE/noarch/repodata.json

The changes needed to be made will mostly affect `conda/core/index.py` and `fetch_repodata`.  Right now, we assume a `404` response on `/noarch/repodata.json` is ok.  We simply need to flip the assumption, and also make sure we have good error messaging for people with their own repos.

issue_title: conda clean -p should remove duplicate pacakge when there is multiple cache
issue_body:
We have an environment where we have a local installation of anaconda shared by many user on each computer. If user create an env and the shared installation do not contain the most up to date version of the packages, they will download new packages in there home. When we update later the shared conda installation, we end up with some packages duplicated in the home under "~/.conda/pkgs" and the in shared installation.

"conda clean -p" should update existing user env to point to the shared installation and delete from there home the duplicate packages.

This would remove much disk access to the home directory that is remote.

I don't know a good/easy enough manual way of doing this. Having this would help in the meantime.

I think the manual way to do this cleanly is:
- Find ourself which packages they have duplicate (very hard, each user create env at different time)
- ask user to uninstall the duplicate packages from all there env
- then do "conda clean -a"
- then reinstall the duplicate packages.

Do you think of a better way of doing this?

issue_title: git compile fails when curl is installed
issue_body:
I just discovered this obscure bug that I'm unable to compile git (I don't have root on some of my Linux servers, that's why I do that) when the `curl` package is installed, due to some C_PATH problem caused by that:

``` bash
imap-send.c: In function ‘setup_curl’:
imap-send.c:1428: error: ‘CURLOPT_LOGIN_OPTIONS’ undeclared (first use in this function)
imap-send.c:1428: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only once
imap-send.c:1428: error: for each function it appears in.)
imap-send.c:1428: warning: type defaults to ‘int’ in declaration of ‘_curl_opt’
make: *** [imap-send.o] Error 1
```

This is probably explained by this:
http://git.661346.n2.nabble.com/Compiling-2-4-0-doesn-t-seem-to-honour-C-INCLUDE-PATH-td7629976.html

and/or by the harshly different versions that exist on this system?:

``` bash
klay6683 at maven-iuvs-itf in ~/src/iuvs (master●●)
$ /usr/bin/curl --version
curl 7.19.7 (x86_64-redhat-linux-gnu) libcurl/7.19.7 NSS/3.14.3.0 zlib/1.2.3 libidn/1.18 libssh2/1.4.2
Protocols: tftp ftp telnet dict ldap ldaps http file https ftps scp sftp
Features: GSS-Negotiate IDN IPv6 Largefile NTLM SSL libz

klay6683 at maven-iuvs-itf in ~/src/iuvs (master●●)
$ curl --version
curl 7.43.0 (x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu) libcurl/7.43.0 OpenSSL/1.0.1k zlib/1.2.8
Protocols: dict file ftp ftps gopher http https imap imaps pop3 pop3s rtsp smb smbs smtp smtps telnet tftp
Features: IPv6 Largefile GSS-API Kerberos SPNEGO NTLM NTLM_WB SSL libz TLS-SRP UnixSockets
```

Anyhow, this can be avoided by creating an environment that does **NOT** have `curl` installed (confirmed).
Can this be somehow avoided for the case when the `curl` package is installed?

Edit: System: Linux, CentOS6

issue_title: Log the index name when a field_stat filter query raises an exception
issue_body:
### Expected Behavior
Sometimes, due to misconfiguration or bad data in Elasticsearch, the "age" filtertype with "field_stats" source may raise an exception.  In my case, the exception was caused by an index with no documents, resulting in the field_stats aggregation returning no min/max values for the designated field.  When this happens, the system should log the name of the index which resulted in the failed aggregation query so that the user can troubleshoot the issue.

### Actual Behavior
The system logs the exception,  but not the index which was being processed at the time:
```
13:33:03,954 DEBUG          curator.indexlist _get_field_stats_dates:263  RESPONSE: {'took': 7, 'timed_out': False, '_shards': {'total': 5, 'successful': 5, 'skipped': 0, 'failed': 0}, 'hits': {'total': 0, 'max_score': 0.0, 'hits': []}, 'aggregations': {'min': {'value': None}, 'max': {'value': None}}}
13:33:03,954 DEBUG          curator.indexlist _get_field_stats_dates:267  r: {'min': {'value': None}, 'max': {'value': None}}
13:33:03,954 ERROR                curator.cli                    run:184  Failed to complete action: close.  <class 'TypeError'>: int() argument must be a string, a bytes-like object or a number, not 'NoneType'
```

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
1.  Create an Elasticsearch index named `my-empty-index` with no documents
2. Configure a Curator action using an age/field_stats filter like:
```yaml
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    description: "Delete old indices"
    options:
      timeout_override: 300
      disable_action: False
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: my-empty-index
      - filtertype: age
        direction: older
        source: field_stats
        field: '@timestamp'
        stats_result: max_value
        unit: days
        unit_count: 2
```
3. Run the curator action

### Specifications

  - Version: 5.4.1
  - Platform: Mac OSX
  - Subsystem: ???


## Context (Environment)
I'm using a field_stats age filter to select indices for closing/deletion in a production ES cluster.  Sometimes empty indexes are created due to an unrelated process which match the other filters in my filter list.  When this happens, the Curator action fails because the field_stats aggregation returns no values.

## Detailed Description
I propose, at a minimum, that the system should log the name of the index being processed when this type of error occurs.  Even better, it would be nice to have a filter option to ignore indexes that fail the aggregation query for one reason or another.

issue_title: Cannot change replica count on closed index
issue_body:
Beginning with ES 1.6, this behavior—which was previously allowed—is now prevented.  An error message results:

```
org.elasticsearch.ElasticsearchIllegalArgumentException: Can't update [index.number_of_replicas] on closed indices [[test_index]] - can leave index in an unopenable state
```

As this _can leave index in an inopenable state,_ this behavior should be prevented for all versions of ES.

issue_title: Getting 'No "add" or "remove" operations' when ignore_empty_list is True
issue_body:
### Expected Behavior
<!--- Tell us what should happen -->
Curator action ends with success when `ignore_empty_list: True` and no aliases are to be added/removed.

### Actual Behavior
<!--- Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
Curator fails with 
```
ERROR     Failed to complete action: alias.  <class 'curator.exceptions.ActionError'>: No "add" or "remove" operations
```
at https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/v5.4.1/curator/actions.py#L116

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
<!--- Provide a link to a live example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Include code to reproduce, if relevant -->
actions/add-alias.yml:
```
---
actions:
  remove1:
    action: alias
    description: "Remove indices to be closed from aliases."
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: True
      name: logstash-app.xxx
    remove:
      filters:
        - filtertype: pattern
          kind: prefix
          value: "logstash-app.yyy-"
        - filtertype: age
          source: name
          timestring: '%Y-%m'
          direction: older
          unit: days
          unit_count: 31
```
### Specifications

  - Version: elasticsearch-curator-v5.4.1
  - Platform: linux
  - Subsystem: ubuntu


## Context (Environment)
<!--- How has this issue affected you? What are you trying to accomplish? -->
<!--- Providing context helps us come up with a solution that is most useful in the real world -->
https://discuss.elastic.co/t/curator-alias-add-not-working-if-remove-list-is-empty/73549
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Detailed Description
<!--- Provide a detailed description of the change or addition you are proposing -->
I ran into this issue (https://discuss.elastic.co/t/curator-alias-add-not-working-if-remove-list-is-empty/73549) with managing aliases and solved it by separating add and remove to independent actions

Now I'm getting "No "add" or "remove" operations" on a remove operation even though ignore_empty_list: True

Quick debug in the curator code shows the action is not properly ignoring empty list of aliases to create and remove.
https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/v5.4.1/curator/actions.py#L116

Will send an illustrative PR soon.

issue_title: Issues with streaming_bulk when literally streaming
issue_body:
```
for success, info in streaming_bulk(es, launchConsumer(consumer), chunk_size=500, max_chunk_bytes=104857600):
                                if not success:
                                        print info
```
For streaming data, acts like just another bulk process.
When trying to insert data from a live stream, if the chunk doesn't fill up to 500, the actions aren't processed, and we're left with missing data.
That kind of defeats the purpose of having a streaming bulk api given that there's no real way for us to know if the data we get is in orders of 500 or 600.
eg: I got an inital load of 12804

With chunk size =500, I get 12500 ingested and 304 remaining.
With chunk size = 2, its too slow and isn't usable.

issue_title: [WebApps] [Discussion] Could the runtime list be more "real-time"?
issue_body:
### Description

Node 8 support was [just deployed](https://github.com/Azure/app-service-announcements/issues/5) to Web Apps (on Windows), and I noticed that calling `az webapp list-runtimes` doesn't reflect that fact (`node|7.7` is the latest Node version displayed). Additionally, Node 6.10 has been available in Linux Web Apps for a while, but running `az webapp list-runtimes --linux` displays `node|6.9` as the latest.

It would be ideal if users could rely solely on this command to understand the set of available runtimes, as opposed to needing to keep up with blogs/[Twitter](https://twitter.com/davidebbo/status/870693592557273088)/GitHub/etc. in order to find out that something is available.

Additionally, in the case of Linux runtimes, it's kind of strange that it states that the list is an "offline" copy, and that you can visit `https://aka.ms/linux-stacks` for the full list. Not only does that seem fairly inconvenient, but the list of available runtimes isn't even up-to-date on that page either! So the user would navigate to the page, only to get the exact same list. I sent a [PR](https://github.com/Microsoft/azure-docs/pull/1898) to update that page 3 days ago, and haven't seen much traction, so while we could try to keep the docs up to date, it just feels like having a really realiable `az webapp list-runtimes` command would be a lot nicer.

---

issue_title: _deploy_arm_template_core not working
issue_body:
We've been passing ```params=open('params.json').read()``` to ```_deploy_arm_template_core``` as parameters, however after ```_merge_parameters``` was added (https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/src/command_modules/azure-cli-resource/azure/cli/command_modules/resource/custom.py#L159) it blows up with: 

```
json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting property name enclosed in double quotes: line 1 column 2 (char 1)
```

The reason is that in the ```_merge_parameters``` we try to iterate through the list and only then do ```json.loads``` - we should do ```json.loads``` before entering the loop.

issue_title: conditions JSON format required is not syntactically correct JSON
issue_body:
### Description
**Outline the issue here:**

The only JSON which the Azure cloud will accept for creating a Monitoring rule is as below:

{
    "dataSource": {
      "odata.type": "Microsoft.Azure.Management.Insights.Models.RuleMetricDataSource",
      "resourceUri": "/subscriptions/your_subscription/resourceGroups/your_resource_group/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/hcm99mon01",
      "metricName": "Percentage CPU",

    "threshold" : 60,
    "operator": "GreaterThan",

}

The comma after  "metricName": "Percentage CPU",

is not correct JSON as there are no items following the comma.

issue_title: `az role assignment create` keeps asking for `az account set` to be run
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When I run `az role assignment create`, it asks me to run  `az account set`.  However, even after setting the account (and logging out and logging back in), I get the same error.

**To Reproduce**
```
# Try to run role assignment
az role assignment create --role=Contributor --scope=/subscriptions/resourceGroups/${RG} --assignee ${SPN_CLIENT_ID}
# Result:  Please run 'az account set' to select active account.

az account set -s ${SUBSCRIPTION_ID}

# Try to run role assignment again
az role assignment create --role=Contributor --scope=/subscriptions/resourceGroups/${RG} --assignee ${SPN_CLIENT_ID}
# Result again:  Please run 'az account set' to select active account.

```

issue_title: [Client] Incorrect handling of '..' in url path.
issue_body:
## Long story short

When making a request with a ClientSession object:
If a requested path contains a path level that is `..` it is not translated by removing the previous level from the path.

## Expected behaviour

Example: 
A request to `http://mysite.com/lvl1/lvl2/../file.tar.gz` should be translated to `http://mysite.com/lvl1/file.tar.gz` before sending.

## Actual behaviour

The request is sent to `http://mysite.com/lvl1/lvl2/../file.tar.gz` 

## Steps to reproduce

```py
import asyncio
import aiohttp

@asyncio.coroutine
def main():
    session = aiohttp.ClientSession()
    resp = yield from session.get('https://pypi.python.org/simple/aiohttp-swagger/../../packages/f1/db/0d22688d79b5de9fc325c5438a0b036bca9d711f80190aa2308f7a3942ad/aiohttp-swagger-1.0.0.tar.gz')
    print(resp.status)
    session.close()

asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(main())

```

In contrary with wget : 

```sh
wget https://pypi.python.org/simple/aiohttp-swagger/../../packages/f1/db/0d22688d79b5de9fc325c5438a0b036bca9d711f80190aa2308f7a3942ad/aiohttp-swagger-1.0.0.tar.gz
```
will result in

```sh
--2017-06-21 14:52:30--  https://pypi.python.org/packages/f1/db/0d22688d79b5de9fc325c5438a0b036bca9d711f80190aa2308f7a3942ad/aiohttp-swagger-1.0.0.tar.gz
Resolving pypi.python.org (pypi.python.org)... 151.101.112.223, 2a04:4e42:1b::223
Connecting to pypi.python.org (pypi.python.org)|151.101.112.223|:443... connected.
HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 200 OK
```
Note that it translates the url path before sending the request.

## Your environment

Ubuntu16.04 amd64 Python3.5 aiohttp 2.1.0

issue_title: Support application coroutine factory in web.run_app and gunicorn worker.
issue_body:
# Summary

It should be something like:
```
async def make_app():
    app = web.Application()
    await setup_app(app)
    return app
```
`web.run_app(make_app)` for `run_app` and `gunicorn script:make_app` for Gunicorn.

# Motivation

We encourage stop passing a loop instance to application.
But the app still needs a loop for it's functionality.
For this we have `app._init_loop(loop)` method which is called imperceptible by `run_app()` and Gunicorn worker. It at least very tricky. Other libraries like `aiohttp-devtool` should also call `_init_loop` internally and so on.

But creation application object inside a coroutine removes the need for extra loop initializer because the loop is already here implicitly.

To do it we need an asynchronous callback to create an application.

# Implementation details

Both parameter for `web.run_app()` and Gunicorn entry point should be either `web.Application` entry or a *async function* which accepts no parameters.
In the last case aiohttp calls this function, gets coroutine back and runs the coroutine. Result is and `web.Application` instance.

# Future improvements

Later we could support [async context manager](https://docs.python.org/dev/library/contextlib.html#contextlib.asynccontextmanager) as factory:
```
@contextlib.asynccontextmanager
async def make_app():
    app = web.Application()
    await init()
    try:
        yield app
    finally:
        await cleanup()
```
While cleanup task could be solved by adding `app.on_cleanup` signal handler maybe somebody prefer more explicit approach.

issue_title: GraphQL support
issue_body:
I built a GraphQL component for `aiohttp` named [aiohttp-graphql](https://github.com/dfee/aiohttp-graphql) (i.e. `pip install aiohttp-graphql`).

The premise is simple: Just provide a schema and attach the view to the route:

```
from aiohttp_graphql import GraphQLView
from .schema import Schema

# ... get an app from somewhere (i.e. app = web.Application())

GraphQLView(app, schema=Schema)
```

And then you'll be able to access both **GraphQL** and **Graph*i*QL** on `/graphql`. There are many configuration options, so RTFD.

I just wanted to put this here for future users. Thanks `aio-libs` folks for maintaining good libraries to build on top of. Cheers –

issue_title: More portable css styles for embedding notebooks in HTML
issue_body:
I understand how https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/179bb24fbf79d153812858126127a91431da3319/notebook/static/style/style.less is a pretty clean set off styles for notebooks, but the styles are not particularly portable. If you intend on exporting a notebook to (static) HTML to embed it in an arbitrary webpage, and import the styles [from the CDN](https://cdn.jupyter.org/notebook/latest/style/style.min.css), you are pretty likely to import styles that conflict with the rest of the webpage. This is most easily run into if that webpage does not use this version of Bootstrap, but that is not the only conflict. For example, common elements like `body`, `a`, `#header` and `.nav` have styles, all of which would very likely conflict with a surrounding page.

Someone wishing to embed a notebook could alter their site's own styles to not conflict, for example by including more classes (for things like `body`) and adding class / id prefixes (such as `mysite-nav`), or by including many strategic uses of `!important`, but if not already done, those solutions would pose a substantial hurdle to be able to embed a notebook in a pre-existing site.

This repo's own styles could be made more portable (for example by using prefixing), but I'm not sure what can be done about Bootstrap's inclusion. Is there a more portable version of Bootstrap? How much of bootstrap is really used? Even if this repo was more portable without changing the use of Bootstrap's code, that would be a great improvement. I suspect most important conflicting styles are actually in this repo directly anyway.

If the CDN had more portable styles, notebooks could be more easily embedded in arbitrary webpages.

This is motivated by https://github.com/baldwint/lektor-jupyter/issues/2.

------
Two impractical alternatives are to embed the HTML an iframe by either

1. making the `src` point to the notebook that is it's own page, which requires the ability to author a page like this (which isn't guaranteed if you don't have edit access to the server, e.g. if you're just using a CMS), or
2. putting _the entire notebook_ in a [`srcdoc` string](https://www.w3schools.com/tags/att_iframe_srcdoc.asp), which would probably be horrible, if possible.

issue_title: Notebook can only be used on root URL directory /
issue_body:
All of the links in notebook are hardcoded to the root / of the URL.

So basically, it expects that the notebook is being run at: localhost:8888/

I have been trying to tunnel notebook through a nginx reverse proxy, so that I can access the notebooks at: localhost/p/

However, this does not work, as notebook does not acknowledge that it is being loaded at localhost/p/ as the base URL.

For example, when I try to load localhost/p/, notebook automatically redirects me to localhost/tree (instead of localhost/p/tree). Where localhost/p/api should work, notebook will only call localhost/api.

This means that notebook is impossible to incorporate into a larger system, as it demands a number of very important directories! It demands /static, /api, /custom, among others.

If notebook removed the leading "/" from its calls, making it location agnostic, then it would be easier to incorporate it into a larger system. e.g. localhost/p/ for notebook, localhost/r/ for rstudio, localhost/prod/ for production shiny apps, etc etc.

issue_title: issues with arange
issue_body:
For testing purposes I tried passing `np.arange`-ed data through the gradients and stumbled upon this weird issue:

```
import autograd
import autograd.numpy as np
import numpy as _np

f = lambda x: np.sum(x)
g = autograd.grad(f)
print(g(np.ones(4))) # okay
print(g(_np.ones(4))) # okay
print(g(np.array([1, 1, 1, 1])))  # error
print(g(_np.array([1, 1, 1, 1]))) # error
print(g(np.arange(4))) # error
```

**upd**: got it, for others who might stuble upon it - the issue is in the fact that [1, 1, 1, 1] are *int*'s, not *float*'s

issue_title: More array indexing problems
issue_body:
Hi, so related to #159 I have another problem. Let me try to give a minimal example:
```
from __future__ import division, print_function, absolute_import

import autograd
import autograd.numpy as np
from autograd.numpy import exp, log, sqrt


labels = np.arange(4)
superpixels = np.vstack([labels]*5)

def blowup(param):
    rval = np.where(superpixels == labels[0], param[0], 0)
    for i in range(1, labels.size):
        rval = rval + np.where(superpixels == labels[i], param[i], 0)
    return rval

def problematic(param):
    return np.sum(exp(blowup(param)))
```
 Then `autograd.grad(problematic)(labels/2)` fails with `TypeError: only length-1 arrays can be converted to Python scalars`.  This was my first try, using where instead of assigning to subarrays. Using another definition for `blowup`:

```
def blowup(param):
    rval = np.sum(np.dstack([(superpixels == labels[i]) * param[i] 
                                             for i in range(labels.size)]), 2)

### What did you do?
I forked the repository and tried running the test suite to make sure that my environment is set up correctly. I used a new Last.fm account that had no scrobbled tracks yet.

The test for test_user_top_tracks failed.

### What did you expect to happen?
Well, I expected it to succeed.

### What actually happened?
```
_________________TestPyLastUser.test_user_top_tracks _____________________________

self = <tests.test_user.TestPyLastUser testMethod=test_user_top_tracks>

    def test_user_top_tracks(self):
        # Arrange
        lastfm_user = self.network.get_user(self.username)
    
        # Act
        things = lastfm_user.get_top_tracks(limit=2)
    
        # Assert
>       self.helper_two_different_things_in_top_list(things, pylast.Track)

tests/test_user.py:223: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 
tests/test_pylast.py:112: in helper_two_different_things_in_top_list
    self.assertEqual(len(things), 2)

Either make sure that the test checks that `things` actually contains two things before calling `self.helper_two_different_things_in_top_list`, or scrobble a different test track before running this test so any test account will have at least two top tracks.

Dear all,

as mentioned on the mailing list, I have been working on a GeoMesa kit to provide basic GIS functionality.

It currently uses shapely to create arbitrary shapes for agents and patches (agents that do not move). Shapes can also be imported from GeoJSON. 
Adapting any shape-to-shape interaction implemented in shapely will be straight-forward.

Corvin

issue_title: Example price_range facet broken with elastic search backend
issue_body:
I tried switching haystack's backend to elastic search, but the the `price_range` facet stopped working. I'm not sure if this is a bug with haystack, maybe an inconsistency between the solr and haystack backend, or if it's specific to Oscar.

I had to dig deep into haystack's code to figure out what's going on. Basically, some `kwargs` are passed:

```python
{u'query_facets': [
    (u'price_exact', '[0 TO 20]'),
    (u'price_exact', '[20 TO 40]'),
    (u'price_exact', '[40 TO 60]'),
    (u'price_exact', '[60 TO 80]'),
    (u'price_exact', '[80 TO 100]'),
    (u'price_exact', '[100 TO *]')],
}
````

```python
# haystack.backends.elasticsearch_backend.ElasticsearchSearchBackend.build_search_kwargs
        if query_facets is not None:
            kwargs.setdefault('facets', {})

            for facet_fieldname, value in query_facets:
                kwargs['facets'][facet_fieldname] = {
                    'query': {
                        'query_string': {
                            'query': value,
                        }
                    },
                }
```

What this means is that if there are multiple query_facets with the same facet_fieldname, only the last facet is actually passed to elasticsearch.

I'm not sure what the best way around this is. Maybe we could munge the facet_fieldnames. Something like this?

```python
{u'query_facets': [
    (u'price_exact__0', '[0 TO 20]'),
    (u'price_exact__1', '[20 TO 40]'),
    (u'price_exact__2', '[40 TO 60]'),
    (u'price_exact__3', '[60 TO 80]'),
    (u'price_exact__4', '[80 TO 100]'),
    (u'price_exact__5', '[100 TO *]')],
}
```

issue_title: unable to run after upgrading to .net 2.0 => 2.1 [terminating with uncaught exception of type EEFileLoadException*]
issue_body:
# terminating with uncaught exception of type EEFileLoadException*

when in ( visual studio code [mac os x] ) I go debug -->  run
It never runs, everything fails with:  terminating with uncaught exception of type EEFileLoadException*

All I have done is upgraded 2.0 -> 2.1   changed launch.json  to the following:

"program": "${workspaceFolder}/bin/Debug/netcoreapp2.1/NetSo.dll",

Update nuget

removed DotNetCliToolReference  as they are no longer needed   ( now part of SDK )

Run  dotnet build  ( success )

and then run  

# General

I get no other output, as this seems to be more of a fail to launch issue, than a code issue
especially as no code changes have taken place,  and no code exceptions are thrown.   This seems more like that MACOS  wont run the new core framework.

I have attached the output from OSX  for clarity.  Any advice would be much appreciated.

[core-crash.mac.txt](https://github.com/dotnet/core/files/1903297/core-crash.mac.txt)

issue_title: Incorrect links in download archives
issue_body:
The two links to download the Windows installers on this page:

https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/release-notes/download-archives/1.0-preview2-download.md

Are actually pointing to the downloads for the binaries (you can see that the URLs are the same in both columns).

With a bit of trail and error I found what I believe to be the correct URLs for the installers:

- https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=809123 for DotNetCore.1.0.0-SDK.Preview2-x86.exe
- https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=809122 for DotNetCore.1.0.0-SDK.Preview2-x64.exe

issue_title: Calling an VB.Net String-functions (like Left(string),Right(string),Space(string),Trim(string)) in a .NET Core project throwing Runtime exceptions
issue_body:
# Calling an VB.Net String-functions (like Left(string),Right(string),Space(string),Trim(string)) in a .NET Core project throwing Runtime exceptions



# General

We have a .Net core project and want to call a old vb.net method. The method have some string functions like Left,right,space and trim . We added the DLL as a reference . But in runtime we are getting method not found exception.

like
Method not found: 'System.String Microsoft.VisualBasic.Strings.Left(System.String)'.'
Method not found: 'System.String Microsoft.VisualBasic.Strings.Space(System.String)'.'
Method not found: 'System.String Microsoft.VisualBasic.Strings.Right(System.String)'.'

Any help\advice is most appreciated because we might not changes the old custome dll code which have dependencies with other systems.

For some issues, you will get a quicker and better response if you file it at a more specific [.NET repo](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/Documentation/core-repos.md). For example, if the problem is with ASP.NET Core, you are encouraged to use the [aspnet/home repo](https://github.com/aspnet/home/issues).

issue_title: Long domain name fails with error "string too long"
issue_body:
Hello there,
I have a domain name which, with tld is 66 characters long. It is 62 characters long excluding the .com
I have obtained certificates successfully for other domains, but trying to obtain a certificate for the long one fails with:

An unexpected error occurred:
Error: [('asn1 encoding routines', 'ASN1_mbstring_ncopy', 'string too long')]
Please see the logfiles in /var/log/letsencrypt for more details.

Please support longer domain names.

issue_title: Augeas 1.4 is still broken (doesn't like mod_rewrite and regular expressions)
issue_body:
I've identified another issue with augeas.

I already know about #981 and I was able to patch my own system to avoid that issue; However, it seems that even the new httpd lens fails when a config file includes regular expressions.

I have 11 config files in my <sites-enabled> folder, and augeas reports “syntax errors” for exactly those files which make use of mod_rewrite. mod_rewrite is an apache module which uses regular expressions for configuration.

At this point, I would suggest that we patch the lens ourselves as we wouldn't want to wait for a new version of augeas.

Please ensure that the values for --apache-handle-sites and --apache-server-root are correct for your environment.. Skipping.

All renewal attempts failed. The following certs could not be renewed:
  /etc/letsencrypt/live/noah2.isnot.org/fullchain.pem (failure)

It seems that we're not taking system specific paths into account well enough, leading us to look for sites-enabled on centos which doesn't typically have that

issue_title: -bash: ./letsencrypt: Is a directory
issue_body:
I'm trying follow [User Guide — Let's Encrypt 0.2.0.dev0 documentation](https://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/using.html#manual), yet I'm getting following while trying to run `./letsencrypt`:

```
[alexus@j ~/letsencrypt]$ git pull
Already up-to-date.

Is producer.flush() a must?
issue_body:
My multi-threaded producer doesn't seem to be sending any messages if flush is NOT included in the end. This is my script:
```
conf = {'bootstrap.servers': 'localhost:9092', 
             'queue.buffering.max.messages': 1000000,

issue_title: dateutil.parse.parse() rejects bytearray as input
issue_body:
```
>>> import dateutil.parser
>>> dateutil.parser.parse(b'17 Jan 2017')
datetime.datetime(2017, 1, 17, 0, 0)
>>> dateutil.parser.parse(bytearray(b'17 Jan 2017'))
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 1168, in parse
    return DEFAULTPARSER.parse(timestr, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 556, in parse
    res, skipped_tokens = self._parse(timestr, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 675, in _parse
    l = _timelex.split(timestr)         # Splits the timestr into tokens
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 192, in split
    return list(cls(s))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 61, in __init__
    '{itype}'.format(itype=instream.__class__.__name__))
TypeError: Parser must be a string or character stream, not bytearray
```

Any function which can operate on a `bytes` ought to be able to operate on a `bytearray`---unless something has specifically been done to prevent that, as in `dateutil.parser.parse()`. When `bytes` works, it's unexpected that `bytearray` would fail. It would be better to accept `bytearray`s as well.

issue_title: Create a friendlier Message class

It would be really convenient to have a custom `Message` class. Considering the usage of Telegram's `Message` is widespread, its attributes should be preserved (so we shouldn't have a `.message` attribute to access the original message as it is often used to access the text). Subclassing it seems like the way to go. In addition:

* `reply` (and `respond`?) methods, of course returning the sent message.
* `download_media` method.
* `reply_to` property to access the reply.

* `from_user`/`chat` and its input variants (via bot too?).
* A way to easily access the entities' text.

issue_title: deciding most appropriate path for windows compatibility for cwltool and schema-salad
issue_body:
Hi As I would be working on windows compatibility, I need some help :smiley:   
Current understanding according to me:  
1. Some of our `CommandLineTool` uses `baseCommand` like `echo` and `cat`. These are unix tools only available in linux system.  
2. We can run Linux container on a windows 10 machine. https://stefanscherer.github.io/run-linux-and-windows-containers-on-windows-10/
3. Docker support for windows server is only from windows server 2016 onwards. https://blog.docker.com/2016/09/dockerforws2016/


 For us providing windows support means, a user can develop a workflow on his windows machine. On `run` if the workflow uses unix utilities/tools, then in that case these workflow should run in a Linux docker container or alternatively we can make it default that all workflows should run in a  Linux container. 
We will be using Appveyor CI for testing and as some of our unit tests uses unix tools like `cat` on testing, I guess we need to provide a Linux container where they can use these tools.
Appveyor has a Visual Studio 2017 image(runs windows server 2016) and [supports docker](https://www.appveyor.com/docs/build-environment/)  But as I read somewhere this Docker support only applies to windows containers and not Linux Containers. I raised a [issue](http://help.appveyor.com/discussions/questions/4051-creating-linux-docker-container-inside-windows-server-2016-visual-studio-2017-image) with appveyor to confirm it.  
I also found a `Linuxkit for windows` released last month that should allow [linux container on windows server 2016](https://thenewstack.io/finally-linux-containers-really-will-run-windows-linuxkit/) but I am not sure if Appveyor already supports it or not.  
So if you have any idea how else we can test these builds, that would be awesome.  
In the meantime what I am can do is, setup docker and linux container on my windows machine. Start working on other issues that are preventing us from windows support and for running test cases, use `--default-container` flag to generate a linux container and run test cases there. 
I talked to Janneke, she is one of my mentor and potential user of windows support and for her use case running workflows in a linux container on windows machine is good.

issue_title: recent commit breaks use of exclude with marshmallow-sqlalchemy
issue_body:
Commit 76e8c91e3decfa4f01ee3c48d71995d2e0a55a27 breaks our use of `exclude` in the Meta of a sqlalchemy-backed model schema.

For example:

```python
class CustomerSchema(BaseSchema):
    class Meta(BaseSchema.Meta):
        model = Customer
        exclude = ('orders',)
```

Where `Customer` is a sqlalchemy model and `orders` is a relation from another table. Putting the exclude in the Meta prevents marshmallow-sqlalchemy from creating the Relation field for `orders` into CustomerSchema. However, with the above commit which adds a check on the excludes, it'll now throw an exception because 'orders' doesn't exist in the schema (because it was excluded!)

How should this be resolved? I could instead use `fields=()` in the Meta and explicitly list out the fields I want instead of using `exclude`, but that's more maintenance overhead (we have to remember to update the fields list if a column is added to the model)

issue_title: functions in turicreate.extensions can be directly imported by ‘import name’ outside of the turicreate namespace
issue_body:
Moving an internal bug report here; unverified. 

After importing turicreate, functions in turicreate.extensions can be directly imported by ‘import name’ outside of the turicreate namespace. This can collide with and take precedence over local user-created packages if the names are the same.  However, these should not Turi submodules and functions should not be implicitly importable without specifying the full turicreate namespace.

Steps To Reproduce:
On a system in a directory with a dummy plot.py file:

Running:
import plot
help(plot)
# Output:
#Help on module plot:
#
#NAME
#    plot
#
#FILE
#    /Users/minghaoji/socpd-ml-utils/ml13/unate_app/unate_lib/plot.py
#
#FUNCTIONS
#    … 

Versus running:
import turicreate
import plot
help(plot)
# Output:
# Help on function plot in module turicreate.extensions:
# 
# plot(*args, **kwargs)
#     Name: plot
#     Parameters: ['path_to_client', 'x', 'y', 'xlabel', 'ylabel', 'title']

Notes:
This appears to be caused by Turi create creating a python import hook which intercepts import calls and gives precedence to turicreate extensions.

issue_title: Identical sequences of random tags all pick the same choice
issue_body:
If you have multiple `{random}...{/random}` sequences and they all look 100% identical (same words in the same order, etc.), then only one of them is actually processed (random choice chosen) and the random choice replaces *all* occurrences of the sequence.

Example:

```
- {random}red|blue|green|black|white|gray|pink{/random} {random}red|blue|green|black|white|gray|pink{/random} {random}red|blue|green|black|white|gray|pink{/random}
```

This might yield an answer of "green green green".

The problem is likely because: after the random choice is picked, it does a regexp find/replace on the full original text of the tag with the answer, but it probably does a global replace, also replacing all other matching tag sequences. If you alternate the words in each set the problem is resolved.

issue_title: Use of WARP for display adverstising
issue_body:
Hi Maciej,

I'm here not for an issue but to have your advice on the following. Please bear with me.
I work in the re-marketing domain of Inmobi, a mobile ad-network. I am trying to solve recommendation problem for cold-start users. I wish to use lightFM, which I think is a really great project you have released in open-source.

Historical data I have: 
- supply+context+user features; eg: publisher, ad slot, day of week, user handset details, user's derived features etc. Hence forth these features will be referred to as Uf
- demand features ie features of items whose ads were shown; eg: brand, category, price, discount etc. Hence forth these feature will be referred to as Vf
- metric: CTR (clicks/impressions shown)
- Hence the data format is: Uf*Vf matrix of partially filled CTR values. 
- Note that user ids are not available. Only features are available.

Objective: 
- Show the most relevant product to the user. Prediction at user level is difficult due to latency issues. Hence Uf represent user type and Vf act as item type. Thus the refined objective is: show the most relevant item type to a user type. Traditional methods used are CTR prediction using classification models. 
- Once the best item type is selected for a given user type, we can choose ads of most popular items in that item type cohort to show to this user type. 

Two approaches I have thought of:
1. Use implicit matrix factorization using ALS/SGD, where implicit score = CTR and confidence = #impressions. In my understanding, this will give results similar to traditional classification methods. Eg: http://blog.ethanrosenthal.com/2016/10/19/implicit-mf-part-1/
2. Learning to rank using WARP loss. Intuitively this should given better results as we are concerned with only top k recommendations for each user type. eg: http://blog.ethanrosenthal.com/2016/11/07/implicit-mf-part-2/

Question I am struggling with, while trying to use lightFM:
1. Are there any flaws in above approaches? 
2. In the 2nd approach, how do I model the CTR matrix into implicit preference matrix? Is there something like "confidence" which can be incorporated? (just like in 1st approach)
3. My data doesn't have user id. Can I still employ any of the above approaches using lightFM? In my understanding, I can feed above Uf*Vf matrix as user*item matrix input to lightFM package and that should be fine?

It will be great if you guide me w.r.t. the above approaches and questions. Any other suggestion is most welcome as well.

Thank You 
Kunal Kishore

A couple of potential problems:

Am I reading this wrong? @Calama-Consulting did you have a different interpretation of this when you added the SPA code to pvlib?

issue_title: seq.at without length constraints sometimes produces incorrect models
issue_body:
Using seq.at in equality assertions seems to sometimes result in incorrect models, for example when there is no assertion about the length of the target sequence:

```
(declare-fun xs () (Seq Int))
(assert (= (seq.at xs 0) (seq.unit 42)))
;; Adding the following assertion results in a correct model:
;; (assert (> (seq.len xs) 0))
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

The above program results in the following model:
```
(model 
  (define-fun xs () (Seq Int)
    (as seq.empty (Seq Int)))
)
```

However, `seq.empty` will not satisfy the above assertion. Interestingly, adding a length constraint like `(assert (= (seq.len xs) 0))` still yields `sat` as the result, with the same model. Is this a bug or did I miss some assumption about the use of sequence operations?

issue_title: Invalid model generated without warning
issue_body:
I've been trying to track down an issue I've been getting where Z3 seems to be generating invalid models. I became suspicious when my tool tried to use Z3 generated models in the tool's expression language and finding that they didn't seem to satisfy the constraints I had passed to Z3.

I'm currently using Z3 via its C API.

I thought doing  `Z3_global_param_set("model_validate", "true")` would detect problems like this but no warnings or errors seem to fire. Seeing as I was very suspicious of this I decided to implement manual checking of a `Z3_model` in my tool (see https://github.com/delcypher/klee/blob/z3_debugging/lib/Solver/Z3Solver.cpp#L347 ).

Basically I iterate over the assertions in the solver and try evaluating the expressions using the model. Each assertion should evaluate to `Z3_L_TRUE`. However when running my tool I've come across a scenario where one of the assertions in the model evaluates to false in the provided model.

When this occurs I have my tool dump the query and the model and this is what it shows.

```
Validating model failed:
The expression:
Z3ASTHandle:
(let ((a!1 (concat (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000003)
                   (concat (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000002)
                           (concat (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000001)
                                   (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000000))))))
(let ((a!2 (bvsle #x00000005
                  (to_ieee_bv (fp.add roundNearestTiesToEven
                                      ((_ to_fp 8 24) a!1)
                                      (fp #b0 #x7f #b00000000000000000000000))))))
  (not (bvsle (ite a!2 #x00000000 #x00000001) #x00000000))))
evaluated to 
Z3ASTHandle:
false
But should be true
Solver state:
(declare-fun f_0x607000022ae0 () (Array (_ BitVec 32) (_ BitVec 8)))
(assert (let ((a!1 (concat (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000003)
                   (concat (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000002)
                           (concat (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000001)
                                   (select f_0x607000022ae0 #x00000000))))))
(let ((a!2 (bvsle #x00000005
                  (to_ieee_bv (fp.add roundNearestTiesToEven
                                      ((_ to_fp 8 24) a!1)
                                      (fp #b0 #x7f #b00000000000000000000000))))))
  (not (bvsle (ite a!2 #x00000000 #x00000001) #x00000000)))))

Model:
(define-fun f_0x607000022ae0 () (Array (_ BitVec 32) (_ BitVec 8))
  (_ as-array k!52))
(define-fun k!52 ((x!0 (_ BitVec 32))) (_ BitVec 8)
  (ite (= x!0 #x00000002) #x88
  (ite (= x!0 #x00000000) #x10
  (ite (= x!0 #x00000003) #x7f
  (ite (= x!0 #x00000001) #x80
    #x88)))))

issue_title: Custom LSTM, GRU layers
issue_body: Currently, it's only possible to create "vanilla" RNNs with non-dense input-to-hidden and hidden-to-hidden connections.  The main reason that there's no `CustomLSTMLayer` or `CustomGRULayer` classes is that there wouldn't be a way (as far as I can tell) to stack the weight matrices for efficiency. issue_title: ZipFS fails to open nested file with non-ASCII name
issue_body:
ZipFS seems to fail when reading archived file with non-ASCII name:

```
# -*- encoding: UTF-8
from __future__ import unicode_literals
from fs.zipfs import ZipFS

with ZipFS("test_archive.zip", write=True) as zip_fs:
    zip_fs.settext("Файл", "some content")

with ZipFS("test_archive.zip") as zip_fs:
    fs_pathes=list(zip_fs.walk.files())
    print("FS filenames:", fs_pathes)
    print("Zipfile filenames:", zip_fs._zip.namelist())
    for path in fs_pathes:
        with zip_fs.openbin(path) as handle:
            print("Opened %s" % path)
```

Resulting output (Python2.7, Linux machine, master Pyfilesystem branch):
```
(u'FS filenames:', [u'/\u0424\u0430\u0439\u043b'])
(u'Zipfile filenames:', ['\xd0\xa4\xd0\xb0\xd0\xb9\xd0\xbb'])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "analyze_archive.py", line 16, in <module>
    with zip_fs.openbin(path) as handle:
  File "/home/fresheed/work/fix_registration/pyfilesystem2-master/fs/zipfs.py", line 371, in openbin
    return _ZipExtFile(self, zip_name)
  File "/home/fresheed/work/fix_registration/pyfilesystem2-master/fs/zipfs.py", line 33, in __init__
    self._end = _zip.getinfo(name).file_size
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.7/zipfile.py", line 905, in getinfo
    'There is no item named %r in the archive' % name)
KeyError: "There is no item named u'\\u0424\\u0430\\u0439\\u043b' in the archive"
```

As we can see, zipfile accepts filenames as bytestrings, while Pyfilesystem operates with Unicode. 
Probable solution is:
```
class _ZipExtFile(RawWrapper):

    def __init__(self, fs, name):
        self._zip = _zip = fs._zip
        name=name.encode(fs.encoding) #convert filename using parent fs's encoding
        self._end = _zip.getinfo(name).file_size
        self._pos = 0
        super(_ZipExtFile, self).__init__(_zip.open(name), 'r', name)
```
but I don't sure if it is the only place to change.

issue_title: raw time stamp parsing error
issue_body:
I don't think you are parsing the raw time stamps correctly in [raw.py](https://github.com/utiasSTARS/pykitti/blob/19d29b665ac4787a10306bbbbf8831181b38eb38/pykitti/raw.py#L257). When I try to do this in python 3.7 on macOS, I get the same as when I ignore the fractional seconds:

```python
>>> s = '2011-09-28 12:26:18.089952593'
>>> time.mktime(datetime.datetime.strptime(s[:-4], "%Y-%m-%d %I:%M:%S.%f").timetuple())
1317191178.0
>>> time.mktime(datetime.datetime.strptime(s[:-10], "%Y-%m-%d %I:%M:%S").timetuple())
1317191178.0
```

They are the same!

However, if I just turn the fractional part into a float and add it, I get (I think) the correct answer:

```python
>>> time.mktime(datetime.datetime.strptime(s[:-10], "%Y-%m-%d %I:%M:%S").timetuple()) + float(s[-11:])
1317191186.0899527
```

Looking at the time stamp, I think (with this fractional part) is correct.

issue_body:
I had read the official docs already, but it does make use on `Meta` class.
My case I need to use it as a stand-alone class and instantiate the `serializer` to do `deserialization`
Because of calculation in `Order` and `OrderItem`

`models.py`
```python
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import models
from djchoices import DjangoChoices, ChoiceItem
from djmoney.models.fields import MoneyField

from poinkbackend.apps.commons.models import AbstractTimestamp
from poinkbackend.apps.menus.models import Menu
from poinkbackend.apps.orders.models import Order
from poinkbackend.apps.prices.models import Price


class OrderItem(AbstractTimestamp):
    class OrderItemStatus(DjangoChoices):
        new = ChoiceItem(1)  # First state
        placed = ChoiceItem(2)  # This is mandatory
        cooking = ChoiceItem(3)  # This is optional
        cancelled = ChoiceItem(4)   # Before `placed` can change it

    # For searching purpose
    order = models.ForeignKey(Order, on_delete=models.CASCADE,
                              related_name='order_items', related_query_name='order_items',
                              null=True, blank=True)  # Will enforce relationship in `OrderItemManager`
    # Suppose in the future this menu is cancelled. Not sell anymore
    # Previous entity must be preserved. Therefore snapshot will save the latest state
    menu = models.ForeignKey(Menu, related_name='order_items', related_query_name='order_items',
                             on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True)
    qty = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=1)
    size = models.CharField(max_length=5, choices=Price.MenuSize.choices, default=Price.MenuSize.M)

    # UnitPrice. For serializer purpose. UnitPrice should be static and not evolve over time
    price = MoneyField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default_currency=settings.DEFAULT_CURRENCY)
    status = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(choices=OrderItemStatus.choices, default=OrderItemStatus.new)

    def __str__(self):

```

```python
from rest_framework import serializers

from poinkbackend.apps.order_items.models import OrderItem


class OrderItemSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):

issue_title: Relocation using local symbol
issue_body:
in relocation.py `relocate()` starts with a symbol resolution leading to `resolve_symbol()`

if the symbol has `is_static` set, the function stops, if not it tries to resolve the symbol using its name.

Problems come when there are multiple symbols with the same name which is legit for local symbols (and used in linux kernel modules for instance).

> [STB_LOCAL](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E19683-01/816-1386/chapter6-79797/index.html)
Local symbol. These symbols are not visible outside the object file containing their definition. Local symbols of the same name can exist in multiple files without interfering with each other.

This leads to Relocation having a wrong `resolvedby` attribute set in `resolve()`. 

As we don't see how STB_LOCAL symbols could be resolved by another symbol, we use this quick & dirty patch:
``` diff
@@ -33,7 +33,7 @@ class Relocation(object):
         if self.resolved:
             return True
 
-        if self.symbol.is_static:
+        if self.symbol.is_static or self.symbol.binding == 'STB_LOCAL':
             # A static symbol should only be resolved by itself.
             self.resolve(self.symbol)
             return True
```
Works for us but maybe we are breaking other use cases. 
Not sure exactly how `is_static` is defined but maybe a STB_LOCAL symbol qualifies.

issue_title: entry_state and args
issue_body:
When setting up an entry state with concrete values
```
>>> p = angr.Project("./filename")
>>> s = p.factory.entry_state(addr=main, args=["./filename", "arg1", "arg2"])
```
I expect the stack layout to look something like:
```
(sp)        7ffeff9c | <BV32 0x3>
            7ffeffa0 | char **argv
            7ffeffa4 | char **envp
            7ffeffa8 | ...
```

Instead is looks like (edited for clarity):
```
>>> print s.state.dbg_print_stack(depth=5)
SP = 0x7ffeff9c, BP = 0x00000000
(sp)        7ffeff9c | <BV32 0x3>
            7ffeffa0 | argv[0]
            7ffeffa4 | argv[1]
            7ffeffa8 | argv[2]
            7ffeffac | ...
```
Is this intended, and if so, why?

issue_title: Recursion Depth Exceeded on tables with mutual foreign keys
issue_body:
If you tables with foreign keys to each other, `odo.backends.sql.discover_sqlalchemy_selectable` gets into an infinite loop.  It is reproducible with the following snippet.

    import sqlalchemy as sa
    from odo.backends import sql

    meta = sa.MetaData()

    UserData = sa.Table(
        'userdata',
        meta,
        sa.Column('id', sa.INTEGER(), primary_key=True, nullable=False),
        sa.Column('spouse_id', sa.INTEGER(), sa.ForeignKey('user.id')),
    )

    User = sa.Table(
        'user',
        meta,
        sa.Column('id', sa.INTEGER(), primary_key=True, nullable=False),
        sa.Column('userdata_id', sa.INTEGER(), sa.ForeignKey('userdata.id')),
    )

    sql.discover_sqlalchemy_selectable(User)

issue_title: PX4 ArduPlane  / Raspberry Pi subsequent connect issue (USB)
issue_body:
I am connecting to a PX4 running ArduPlane, I connect via USB from a Raspberry Pi3. My script sends RC overrides and reads the general vehicle state variables (speed, position, mode, RC, etc). I connect with the line: 

stdbuf -oL python flightProgram.py --connect /dev/ttyACM0 | tee -i $LOGPATH/$outFile

flightProgram.py uses the following line: 
vehicle = connect(connection_string, wait_ready=True, rate=20, baud=1500000,vehicle_class=ServoVehicle), 

where ServoVehicle is a superclass of vehicle that keeps track of RC output values as well.

Code here: https://github.com/cheintz/DroneKit_FW_Formation2017/tree/development/flightControl

If I stop and then restart my script, the connection fails. I get zero to maybe 10 re-connects before having to powercycle the Pi and PX4. This is inconvenient as it requires landing.

This problem does NOT occur when I connect my script to a simvehicle.py simulated vehicle in Ubuntu virtual machine. 

Can you all think of anything that would be causing this? Do you think using one of the hardware serial ports on the PX4 and Pi would help?

Thanks,
Chris

issue_title: Support setting yaw rate and yaw acceleration
issue_body:
This feature is needed to preserve a smooth feel in response to target angle change while recording video.  Without it, the scene has a "jerky" feel and the subject move across the field of view instead of staying centered.  Importantly in contrast to upstream discussion that appear to talk about a global setting, this would need to be settable per command.

Some documentation from the various examples in particular; currently this appears to be unsupported but the example documentation might be old and the issues are closed.

### Ardu [documenation](http://ardupilot.org/copter/docs/common-mavlink-mission-command-messages-mav_cmd.html#mav-cmd-condition-yaw) of MAV_CMD_CONDITION_YAW
`The parameters allow you to specify whether the target direction is absolute or relative to the current yaw direction. If the direction is relative you can also (separately) specify whether the value is added or subtracted from the current heading (note that the vehicle will always turn in direction that most quickly gets it to the new target heading regardless of the param3 value).`

`We don’t support controlling the yaw rate, so the param2 value (Sec) is ignored.`

## Dronkekit [examples](http://python.dronekit.io/examples/guided-set-speed-yaw-demo.html)   

### send_global_velocity

        velocity_z, # Z velocity in NED frame in m/s
        0, 0, 0, # afx, afy, afz acceleration (not supported yet, ignored in GCS_Mavlink)
        0, 0)    # yaw, yaw_rate (not supported yet, ignored in GCS_Mavlink)

### condition_yaw

        heading,    # param 1, yaw in degrees
        0,          # param 2, yaw speed deg/s
        1,          # param 3, direction -1 ccw, 1 cw

## Ardu issues 
This is apparently a requested feature upstream. includes ArduPilot/ardupilot#1635, ArduPilot/ardupilot#1758

issue_title: Shuffle mode
issue_body:
Something that would be perfect for frequent playlists is a shuffle mode so it doesn't feel exactly the same every time you open it, with the order of the videos appearing scrambled.

For example, when you open a playlist and `dump` it, there could be a similar `shuffle-dump` command to randomly scramble the order of the videos in the playlist.

Currently running 0.2.8 from pip, sorry if something like this is already in place here on git.

issue_title: Update Documentation
issue_body:
#### Problem description

The project documentation at satpy.readthedocs.io is lacking a lot of information. I'm currently rewriting some of it, but wanted to keep a running public TODO list of what needs to be added or updated. Everyone, feel free to comment ideas and I'll add them to the list. If anyone wants to start one of these tasks, please let me know first.

- [ ] Mention optional dependencies in installation instructions
- [ ] In index mention that although the interfaces aren't stable, satpy is used operationally
- [ ] Add overview page providing a quick summary of each of the main components of satpy: reading, compositing, resampling, enhancing, writing, datasets (DataArrays), and configuration files.
- [ ] Add reader creation tutorial
- [ ] Add writer creation tutorial
- [ ] Add composite creation tutorial
- [ ] Add custom enhancement tutorial
- [ ] Add writer creation tutorial
- [ ] Add various examples of how the Scene can be created
- [ ] Add page for file and reader finding utility function (if not a section to the above)
- [ ] Add tutorial with overlays or other writer customizations

What tutorials go in the docs directly versus go in a jupyter notebook in `pytroll/pytroll-examples`?

issue_title: 运行源代码中的test.py代码，策略里面的init,handle_bar方法没有调用
issue_body:
在test_api()方法里单独调用一行代码run(future_api_config, test_buy_open_code_new)，运行到main.py文件中run（）方法里面  scope = env.strategy_loader.load(env.config.base.strategy_file if source_code is None else source_code, scope)这条语句运行完成后，策略代码没有加载到scope里面，导致user_strategy = Strategy(env.event_bus, scope, ucontext)初始化的时候找不到init,handle_bar方法。具体策略：
def test_buy_open():
    from rqalpha.api import buy_open, subscribe, get_order, ORDER_STATUS, POSITION_EFFECT, SIDE
    def init(context):
        print "test_buy_open ladkfaldsfkjdsalfjldsajf1111111111111111"
        context.f1 = 'P88'
        context.amount = 1
        # context.marin_rate = 10
        subscribe(context.f1)
        context.order_count = 0
        context.order = None

    def handle_bar(context, bar_dict):
        order_id = buy_open(context.f1, 1)
        order = get_order(order_id)
        assert order.order_book_id == context.f1
        assert order.quantity == 1
        assert order.status == ORDER_STATUS.ACTIVE
        assert order.unfilled_quantity == 1
        assert order.unfilled_quantity + order.filled_quantity == order.quantity
        assert order.side == SIDE.BUY
        assert order.position_effect == POSITION_EFFECT.OPEN
test_buy_open_code_new = "".join(inspect.getsourcelines(test_buy_open)[0])

issue_title: Why does SplashTextResponse inherits from TextResponse instead HTMLResponse
issue_body:
Why does `scrapy_splash.reponse.SplashTextResponse` inherits from `scrapy.http.reponse.text.TextResponse`?

I understand that they are identical, and `HTMLResponse` uses `TextResponse` without any change, but they mean different things and Scrapy uses `isinstance` check to distinguish them.

In my case, it causes problem because I have type check for `HTMLResponse` where I expect to get HTML page and use `css` method to extract data from it, but `SplashTextResponse` doesn't pass it.

issue_title: Repeatedly changing exposure of d435 brings down a camera
issue_body:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D435 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 5.9.9.2 | 
| Operating System & Version |  Linux (Ubuntu 16) | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  tegra ubuntu 4.4.38                                        | 
| Platform                                 | NVIDIA Jetson  |
| SDK Version                            |  2.9.1 }                          | 


### Issue Description
We are running a system with two d435 cameras connected to an NVIDIA TX2. We discovered that repeatedly changing exposure on one camera will bring the second camera down.

We used the following bash script to invoke ros param server to change the exposure settings:

`#!/bin/bash`
`realsense2_camera="/sensors/camera_front_forward/realsense2_camera"`
`while :`
`do`
`#loop infinitely`
`rosrun dynamic_reconfigure dynparam set $realsense2_camera rs435_depth_exposure 200`
`sleep 5`
`rosrun dynamic_reconfigure dynparam set $realsense2_camera rs435_depth_exposure 30`
`sleep 5`
`done`

With the latest firmware released by Intel we can get this script to bring down a camera after about 5 hours run time ( interestingly it brings down the second camera down, and not the one on which we change the exposure rates)

With a previous version of firmware we can reproduce this in about two hours with the script above.
  

We have applied the UVC Linux drivers to the NVIDIA Tegra kernel patch as per installation instructions.

issue_title: POTCAR modifications ignored in elastic workflow
issue_body:
## System

* Version: master
* Python version: 3.5.3
* OS version: fedora 25

## Summary

* Modifying the `vasp_input_set` as recommended [here](https://hackingmaterials.github.io/atomate/customizing_workflows.html#use-a-different-potcar) is ignored after the workflow is generated.
* `PAW_PBE Sm_3 07Sep2000` is used by default, I would like to use `PAW_PBE Sm 21Nov2014`

## Example code

```python
from pymatgen.io.vasp.inputs import Poscar
from pymatgen.io.vasp.sets import MPStaticSet
from atomate.vasp.workflows.base.elastic import get_wf_elastic_constant
from fireworks import LaunchPad

lp = LaunchPad.auto_load()
poscar = Poscar.from_file("POSCAR")
structure = poscar.structure
vis_input = MPStaticSet(structure)
vis_input.config_dict['POTCAR']['Sm'] = 'Sm'   # Changed here from Sm_3
wf = get_wf_elastic_constant(structure, vasp_input_set=vis_input)
lp.add_wf(wf)
```

issue_title: Bug: Name of a connection is always the name of the class
issue_body:
```
class BranchesOnClient(relay.Connection):
    pass
```

Based on the implementation of `relay.Connection` I expect the default name to be `BranchesOnClientConnection`.

```
class BranchesOnClient(relay.Connection, name="Foo"):
    pass
```

Now I expect the type name to be `Foo`.

In fact, in both cases the name will be `BranchesOnClient`. This is because `BaseType` sets `_meta.name` to the class name if no `name` argument is passed, and no `name` argument is passed because `relay.Connection` already caught it, assigned it to `_meta`, and removed it from the `**kwargs` dict that is passed on through the ``__init_subclass_with_meta__`` chain.

issue_title: Circular dependencies
issue_body:
Python is not able to resolve circular dependencies for schemas with recursive types.

e.g
```py
  # category_type.py
  from .post_type import Post
  class Category(graphene.ObjectType):
    Post = graphene.List(Post)

  # post_type.py
  from .category_type import Category
  class Post(graphene.ObjectType):
    category = graphene.Field(Category)
```

At runtime, the above fails as the types haven't yet loaded before being used in field definitions. A better option would be to wrap the field definitions in a lambda to be called at resolve time and after the types have been declared.

e.g
```diff
  # category_type.py
-  from .post_type import Post
  class Category(graphene.ObjectType):
-   Post = graphene.List(Post)
+   Post = lambda: graphene.List(Post)

+ from .post_type import Post

  # post_type.py
- from .category_type import Category
  class Post(graphene.ObjectType):
-   category = graphene.Field(Category)
+   category = lambda: graphene.Field(Category)

+ from .category_type import Category
```

cc @Globegitter

issue_title: AsyncIO Executor
issue_body:
I've run into a bug with the AsyncIO Executor when trying to run a query from an application where the event is running. 

I think it's because the executor's implemention of `wait_until_complete` uses `run_until_complete` which starts up the event loop and waits. Since the event loop is already running, it threw an exception when I tried to start it a second time.

issue_title: Native Python enums do not work
issue_body:
Currently using SQL enums works well, unless you use a `enum.Enum` as base for the enum. For example using this model:

```python
class Hairkind(enum.Enum):
    LONG = 'long'
    SHORT = 'short'

class Pet(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'pets'
    id = Column(Integer(), primary_key=True)
    hair_kind = Column(Enum(Hairkind, name='hair_kind'), nullable=False)
```

will fail badly if you try to query the hairKind field:

```
